_comment: Engineering-discovered codex-cli framework issues found while onboarding Codey (codex) onto Instar, May 2026. These are the substantive compat issues found by code-auditing + fixing — the ones the live mentor-tick forensics never sees. Backfilled into the FrameworkIssueLedger via POST /framework-issues/observe so they feed the next-framework onboarding playbook (the framework-limitation + instar-integration-gap buckets generalize). dedupKey is stable so re-running is idempotent.

findings.title: Thread/TopicResumeMap jsonlExists was Claude-only → get() returned null for ALL codex sessions (no resume)
SessionWatchdog was claude-PID/pane-only → an 8.5h-hung codex `exec` job went uncaught
Relay duplicate-reply on codex: MCP retries emit a fresh event id per retry → id-dedup misses it
findings.evidence: PR #573 content-hash dedup; symptom: 2 distinct-id messages 23s apart

findings.title: RateLimitSentinel was codex-blind in BOTH detection (claude pane strings) and recovery (claude-only readJsonlBaseline)
findings.evidence: PR #33 (codex parity v2); account-wide newest-rollout growth signal

findings.title: CompactionSentinel recovery-verification was codex-blind → re-injected recovery xN then false compaction:failed every codex compaction
findings.evidence: PR #593; findNewestRolloutSync codex branch in readJsonlBaseline

findings.title: Codex `exec` runs ONE turn then exits — no native Stop-hook-driven multi-turn loop, so codex can't sustain autonomous multi-turn work
findings.evidence: PR #28; autonomous-stop-hook.sh --codex rides the existing codex Stop group (dark)

findings.title: Codex hook trust-flow does not trust the newly-armed autonomous Stop slot (stop:0:N) → driver registered but unarmed; trusted_hash is opaque
findings.evidence: CMT-826; live-verify on Codey showed stop:0:4 not trusted; must-fix BEFORE enabling codexLoopDriver

findings.title: No codex usage observability — needed a /codex/usage reader of the codex rollout rate-limit windows (claude had none equivalent)
findings.evidence: directive #4a; GET /codex/usage reads codex session rollout primary/secondary windows

findings.title: No model-swap on codex rate-limit exhaustion — auto-swap to a fallback codex model when the secondary window is spent
findings.evidence: directive #4b; ships dark, awaiting confirmed GPT-5.3-Codex-Spark model id + live fall-back proof

findings.title: The instar ship-gate is disproportionately costly for codex agents — codex is LLM-bound for every step (no programmatic fast-path), so the gate stalls a loaded box
findings.evidence: task #25; codex exec spawns ephemeral per-turn processes that narrate intent with no persistent worker

findings.title: Codex emits no SubagentStart/Stop lifecycle events (capability FALSE) → the ReapGuard active-subagent guard is blind for codex
findings.evidence: capabilities.ts:12 SubagentLifecycleHooks:FALSE; verified SessionReaper transcript-growth + positive-idle layers (SessionReaper.ts:242,263) are codex-aware and still protect the session, so the guard is redundant
findings.wontFixReason: Codex declares no subagent lifecycle events by design; the SessionReaper's codex-aware transcript-growth + positive-idle layers already protect a codex session running a subagent, so the per-subagent guard is redundant, not a wrong-reaping risk.
